## pencil
The Pencil MCP server is the editor for .pen design files (web/mobile apps and websites). Use it to read, generate, and validate designs.

.pen files are encrypted: access them only via "pencil" MCP tools — never use Read or Grep on .pen files. Follow each tool's input schema exactly.

Tools: get_editor_state, open_document, get_guidelines, batch_get, batch_design, snapshot_layout, get_screenshot, get_variables, set_variables, find_empty_space_on_canvas, search_all_unique_properties, replace_all_matching_properties, export_nodes.

## plugin:personal-os:notebooklm-mcp
NotebookLM MCP - Access NotebookLM (notebooklm.google.com).

**Auth:** If you get authentication errors, run `nlm login` via your Bash/terminal tool. This is the automated authentication method that handles everything. Only use save_auth_tokens as a fallback if the CLI fails.
**Account Switching:** To switch Google Accounts for the MCP server, run `nlm login switch <profile>` in Bash. The MCP server instantly uses the active default profile.
**Confirmation:** Tools with confirm param require user approval before setting confirm=True.
**Studio:** After creating audio/video/infographic/slides, poll studio_status for completion.

Consolidated tools:
- source_add(source_type=url|text|drive|file, url=..., document_id=..., text=..., file_path=...): Add any source type
- studio_create(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Create any artifact type
- studio_revise: Revise individual slides in an existing slide deck
- download_artifact(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Download any artifact type
- note_create/note_list/note_update/note_delete: Manage notes in notebooks

Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. - update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

---

VietNexus "Innovation OS" (dqplus-hackathon-2026, July 2026): startup↔investor matching app for the Vietnamese ecosystem.

- The design/behavior spec is a bundled prototype at `/mnt/d/Obsidian/outputs/hackathon/VietNexus Onboarding.html` — content is gzip+base64 inside a `__bundler/manifest` script tag; the readable app template/logic is in the `__bundler/template` JSON (extract before reading).
- `frontend/` is Vite + React 18 + GSAP, built 2026-07-18 by parallel Sonnet subagents against a shared contract spec. Dev server proxies `/api/*` → gateway at :3000.
- `backend/gateway` is Express + Sequelize/Postgres: `/auth/register|login|me` (username+password+role founder|investor, JWT), `/profiles` CRUD. No CORS (proxy relied upon). Roles: frontend 'startup' maps to backend 'founder'.
- Match datasets are static in `frontend/src/data/ecosystem.js` — no matching engine exists yet; that's the obvious next build step.
- Consent + Draft/Ready status have no backend columns; they live in localStorage (`vn.session`, `vn.profile.<username>`).

content.text:
Fixed — `https://dqplus.ddns.net` now serves the app, and plain-HTTP requests redirect instead of erroring.

**What was wrong:** The servers were running fine locally. The traffic path is browser → nginx on your VPS (13.250.9.139, HTTPS only) → SSH tunnels back to this machine (`/` → Vite preview :5173, `/api/` → gateway :3000). Two things broke at the edges:

1. **Your 400 error** — nginx only listened on 443 with SSL, so any plain `http://` request hitting it (e.g. `http://dqplus.ddns.net:443` or a non-HTTPS link) got "plain HTTP request was sent to HTTPS port". I added a port-80 server block and an `error_page 497` redirect on the VPS, so both cases now 301-redirect to HTTPS (config backed up first, `nginx -t` passed, reloaded).
2. **A hidden 403** — even over HTTPS, Vite's preview server was rejecting requests because the forwarded `Host: dqplus.ddns.net` wasn't allowed. I added `allowedHosts: ['dqplus.ddns.net']` to both `server` and `preview` in `frontend/vite.config.js` and restarted the preview server.

Verified from here:
- `http://dqplus.ddns.net` → 301 → HTTPS
- `http://dqplus.ddns.net:443` → 301 → HTTPS (the exact case that produced your 400)
- `https://dqplus.ddns.net` → 200, serving the freshly rebuilt bundle
- `https://dqplus.ddns.net/api/auth/me` → gateway responds through the tunnel (expected 401 without a token)

Both servers are still running in the background: gateway on `0.0.0.0:3000`, frontend preview on `0.0.0.0:5173`. The `vite.config.js` change is uncommitted — say the word if you want it committed.

content.text:
Fixed. The problem was `frontend/.env.production` setting `VITE_API_BASE=http://13.250.9.139:5000` — that URL got baked into the production bundle, and the browser refused the call because an HTTPS page can't make plain-HTTP requests (that's the mixed-content block behind the message you saw; the referrer policy line was just noise).

What I changed:

- Set `VITE_API_BASE=/api` in `frontend/.env.production`, so the built app calls the same-origin `/api/...` path. Nginx on the VPS already proxies `/api/` through the tunnel to your gateway, so no CORS or mixed content is involved.
- Rebuilt the frontend and confirmed the old `13.250.9.139` URL is gone from the bundle.

Verified against the live site: `https://dqplus.ddns.net` now serves the new bundle (`index-CrhxZjUU.js`), and `POST https://dqplus.ddns.net/api/auth/login` reaches the gateway — it returns the expected `401 {"error":"Invalid credentials"}` for a fake user, so login should work in the browser now. Try it; a hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) may be needed to drop the cached old bundle.

---

VietNexus "Innovation OS" (dqplus-hackathon-2026, July 2026): startup↔investor matching app for the Vietnamese ecosystem.

- Design/behavior spec: bundled prototype at `/mnt/d/Obsidian/outputs/hackathon/VietNexus Onboarding.html` (gzip+base64 in `__bundler/manifest`; readable template in `__bundler/template` JSON).
- Services (all Express): `backend/gateway` :3000 (auth + profiles, Sequelize, JWT), `backend/matching-engine` :3002 (pgvector cosine + attribute scoring over `extracted_profiles`), `backend/agent/extract` :3003 locally (**3001 in .env.example but Uptime Kuma occupies 3001 on this machine**) — builds `extracted_profiles` rows + embeddings via FPT-cloud OpenAI-compatible API.
- All three share Postgres DB `gateway` (user/pass `dealflow`/`dealflow`) in the sibling project's container `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`. pgvector was apt-installed into that running container on 2026-07-18 — **lost if the container is recreated**; durable fix is switching that compose to `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` (same data volume works).
- Frontend (Vite+React+GSAP) has NO mock data as of 2026-07-18: matches come from the matching engine via vite proxies (`/api/matches`→:3002, `/api/extract`→:3003, `/api`→gateway :3000). Demo-mode fake login removed; session now stores `user.id` (older localStorage sessions are invalidated). Flow: "I'm ready" → save profile → POST /extract/profile → GET matches (404 → auto-extract → retry).
- **Hard blocker**: `OPENAI_API_KEY` in `backend/agent/extract/.env` is empty — extraction 401s until a key is provided, so no matches can be generated.
- No backend exists for the customers/partners/talent match intents (tabs show honest "not connected" notice), outreach-draft generation is a client-side template (`lib/draft.js`), and consent/draft-ready status still live only in localStorage (`vn.profile.<username>`).

content.text:
Done — all three services are now routed through `https://dqplus.ddns.net`, verified live:

| Public path | Service | Result |
|---|---|---|
| `/api/agents/` | AI agent (:3003) | `{"status":"ok","db":"connected"}` |
| `/api/backend/` | backend gateway (:3000) | `{"status":"ok","db":"connected"}` |
| `/api/matches/` | matching engine (:3002) | `{"status":"ok","db":"connected"}` |
| `/api/extract/` | AI agent (:3003) | `{"status":"ok","db":"connected"}` |
| `/api/` (everything else) | gateway (fallback) | `{"status":"ok","db":"connected"}` |

What it took, beyond the nginx edit you asked for:

1. **New reverse tunnels** — only the gateway had a tunnel to the VPS. I added two autossh tunnels: VPS :5002 → local :3003 (agent) and VPS :5003 → local :3002 (matching). Gotcha found on the way: `autossh -f` daemonizes and loses the working directory, so the relative `./hackathonai.pem` path the existing tunnels use silently breaks reconnection — the new tunnels use the absolute key path.
2. **Nginx on the VPS** — added `location` blocks for `/api/agents/`, `/api/backend/`, `/api/matches/`, `/api/extract/` (prefixes are stripped before proxying, so `/api/agents/health` hits the agent's `/health`). The old `/api/` → gateway route stays as fallback, so the current frontend build keeps working unchanged. Config backed up to `default.bak.agents`, `nginx -t` passed, reloaded.
3. **Restarted the gateway** — it had been killed during the service restarts and wouldn't boot: someone's CORS edit in `backend/gateway/src/app.js` used `cors(...)` without requiring it. I added the missing `const cors = require("cors")` and started it again on `0.0.0.0:3000`.

Two notes: I kept `/api/matches` and `/api/extract` as nginx routes (not just the two you named) because those are the paths baked into the deployed frontend bundle — without them, matches would silently 404 in production. And the frontend still calls `/api/auth/...` etc. via the fallback route; if you want it to use `/api/backend/...` explicitly, that's a frontend change + rebuild — say the word.
